title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 40 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: Tu BiShevat
festivals.description: The Jewish New Year for Trees celebrates the blooming of almond trees across Israel with tree-planting ceremonies and nature walks. Families gather to plant trees and enjoy traditional fruits and nuts.

festivals.name: Purim
festivals.description: A joyous Jewish holiday featuring costume parades, festive meals, and street celebrations commemorating the salvation of the Jews in ancient Persia. Parties and celebrations happen throughout the country.

festivals.name: Red Flower Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates the blooming of Israel's iconic red anemones across the Negev desert with guided nature tours and photography expeditions. This annual springtime phenomenon transforms the landscape into a sea of red flowers.

festivals.name: Passover (Pesach)
festivals.description: One of Judaism's most important holidays commemorating the exodus from Egypt, featuring seders, festive meals, and cultural events throughout the country. Many regions offer special Passover programs and family activities.

festivals.name: Jerusalem Wine Festival
festivals.description: Showcases Israel's thriving wine industry with tastings from prestigious local wineries and educational seminars. The festival attracts wine enthusiasts from around the world to discover Israeli varietals.

festivals.name: Remember the Fallen
festivals.description: A national day of remembrance honoring fallen soldiers and victims of terror with ceremonies, parades, and memorial services throughout Israel. The country observes a moment of silence to mark the occasion.

festivals.name: Israeli Independence Day (Yom Ha'atzmaut)
festivals.description: Israel's most significant national holiday celebrating independence with fireworks, parades, concerts, and street parties across the entire country. Every city and town hosts celebrations reflecting Israeli culture and pride.

festivals.name: Shavuot
festivals.description: A major Jewish holiday celebrating the giving of the Torah with festive meals, cheese and dairy foods, and special services. Many kibbutzim host traditional harvest celebrations and cultural events.

festivals.name: Dead Sea Marathon
festivals.description: An international running event held at the lowest point on Earth featuring marathon, half-marathon, and 10K races with stunning desert views. Participants run along the Dead Sea shores in one of the world's most unique locations.

festivals.name: Red Sea Jazz Festival
festivals.description: A world-class jazz festival featuring international and Israeli artists performing on beachfront stages in the tropical resort city of Eilat. The festival draws jazz lovers from across the globe for a week of performances.

festivals.name: White Night Festival
festivals.description: Tel Aviv's summer celebration featuring free concerts, performances, art installations, and cultural events throughout the night until dawn. The entire city stays awake celebrating contemporary Israeli culture.

festivals.name: Acre Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates the historic Old City of Acre with theater, music, dance performances, and art exhibitions in the ancient streets and renovated venues. The festival highlights the city's rich multicultural heritage and artistic talent.

festivals.name: Rosh Hashanah
festivals.description: The Jewish New Year marked by special prayers, festive meals with apples and honey, and family gatherings emphasizing renewal and reflection. Services and celebrations occur in synagogues and communities nationwide.

festivals.name: Yom Kippur
festivals.description: The holiest day in Judaism dedicated to atonement with fasting, prayer services, and family reflection lasting from sunset to sunset. The entire country comes to a standstill as people observe this sacred holiday.

festivals.name: Sukkot
festivals.description: A seven-day harvest festival where families build temporary dwellings (sukkot) and gather for festive meals celebrating the autumn harvest and wilderness wandering. Markets and street fairs feature special foods and traditional activities.

festivals.name: Simchat Torah
festivals.description: A joyous celebration marking the completion and renewal of the Torah cycle with dancing, music, and street parties throughout Jewish communities. Jerusalem's streets come alive with celebrations and traditional processions.

festivals.name: International Marathon
festivals.description: A major international marathon event running through Jerusalem's historic streets and neighborhoods attracting thousands of runners worldwide. The race combines athletic competition with cultural tourism through the ancient city.

festivals.name: Olive Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates the traditional olive harvest season with demonstrations of traditional pressing methods, tastings of fresh oil, and cultural performances. Visitors can participate in harvesting and learn about Palestinian and Israeli olive farming heritage.

festivals.name: Hanukkah
festivals.description: The eight-day Festival of Lights celebrates the rededication of the ancient Temple with menorah lighting, gifts, festive meals, and public celebrations. Each night features lights, music, and traditional fried foods throughout the country.

festivals.name: International Film Festival
festivals.description: One of the world's leading film festivals showcasing Israeli and international cinema with screenings, premieres, and industry events throughout Jerusalem. The festival attracts acclaimed filmmakers and cinephiles from around the globe.

festivals.name: Tsfat Klezmer Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates traditional Klezmer music and Yiddish culture in the mystical hilltop city of Tsfat (Safed) with concerts, workshops, and street performances. Musicians from Israel and abroad gather to celebrate this vibrant musical heritage.

festivals.name: Negev Beer Festival
festivals.description: Showcases Israel's growing craft beer industry with tastings, food pairings, and live entertainment in the Negev desert. Breweries from across Israel present their latest creations in this celebration of Israeli brewing culture.

festivals.name: Tel Aviv Pride Parade
festivals.description: One of the largest pride parades in the Middle East attracting hundreds of thousands of participants for a colorful celebration of LGBTQ+ rights and culture. The entire city celebrates with events, parties, and cultural programs.

festivals.name: Nahal Soreq Wildflower Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates the spring wildflower bloom in the Nahal Soreq nature reserve with guided botanical walks and nature photography. Visitors experience the stunning array of colorful wildflowers in their natural habitat.

festivals.name: International Jewish Film Festival
festivals.description: Presents documentary and narrative films exploring Jewish themes and Israeli culture with screenings across multiple cities including Tel Aviv. The festival features international award-winning films and Israeli productions.

festivals.name: Netanya Festival
festivals.description: A major summer festival on Israel's Mediterranean coast featuring concerts, theater, dance performances, and art exhibitions along the beach. The festival celebrates contemporary Israeli culture with events for all ages.

festivals.name: Caesarea Theater Festival
festivals.description: World-class performances including classical music, opera, and dance held in the ancient Roman amphitheater of Caesarea with the Mediterranean as backdrop. International and Israeli artists perform in this uniquely atmospheric venue.

festivals.name: Haifa Nights Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates Haifa's multicultural character with concerts, performances, fireworks, and family events throughout the waterfront and city neighborhoods. The festival highlights the city's Baha'i heritage and diverse communities.

festivals.name: Golan Wine Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates the Golan Heights' world-renowned wines with tastings from local wineries, vineyard tours, and food pairings in the scenic mountain region. Visitors experience both the wines and stunning volcanic landscape.

festivals.name: Banias Waterfall Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates the natural beauty of Banias waterfall with nature walks, archaeological tours, and water activities in the lush Galilee region. The festival highlights the area's natural springs and historical sites.

festivals.name: Eilat Coral Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates the unique coral ecosystem of the Red Sea with diving exhibitions, marine conservation talks, and underwater photography displays. Visitors learn about protecting one of the world's most vibrant coral reefs.

festivals.name: Jaffa Spring Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates spring in the historic Old City of Jaffa with art exhibitions, street performances, food festivals, and cultural events in ancient alleyways. The festival showcases local artists and the rich cultural heritage of this ancient port.

festivals.name: Mizrahi Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates Middle Eastern and North African Jewish culture with music, dance, traditional foods, and cultural performances. The festival honors the heritage of Mizrahi Jews from Arab countries.

festivals.name: Galilee Culinary Festival
festivals.description: Showcases the diverse cuisines and local produce of the Galilee region with cooking demonstrations, tastings, and farm visits. Local chefs highlight traditional and contemporary Galilee cooking techniques.

festivals.name: Jordan Valley Date Harvest Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates the date harvest season in the Jordan Valley with tasting of fresh dates, local products, and demonstrations of traditional harvesting methods. Visitors experience the region's oldest agricultural tradition.

festivals.name: Samaria Wine Route Festival
festivals.description: Explores boutique wineries and vineyards of the Samaria region with tastings, vineyard tours, and artisanal food pairings. The festival showcases the emerging wine industry in the region's rolling hills.

festivals.name: Ein Gedi Spring Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates the natural springs and oasis of Ein Gedi in the desert with hiking, nature photography, and cultural performances. The festival highlights the unique ecology and ancient history of this desert sanctuary.

festivals.name: Northern Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Features international and Israeli jazz artists performing in the scenic Galilee region with intimate venues and stunning mountain backdrops. The festival celebrates jazz in one of Israel's most beautiful natural settings.

festivals.name: Sderot Craft Beer Festival
festivals.description: A vibrant craft beer celebration featuring local and Israeli breweries with food trucks, live music, and family activities. The festival supports the growing craft beer community in southern Israel.

festivals.name: Petach Tikva Blossom Festival
festivals.description: Celebrates spring blooms throughout Israel's historic first city with floral displays, parades, and family activities. The festival honors Petach Tikva's pioneering role in Israel's agricultural development.
